These constituents include conchicine, conhydrine, and coniine, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to reduce pain and fight infections in individuals suffering from conditions like arthritis and wounds. side_effects: Conchograecum cribbianum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. In some cases, improper use of the plant may lead to dizziness, nausea, and vomiting, while others may experience skin irritation or rashes.
